blocks.content: She was close enough that he could see the pin in her hair and the streetlight in her eyes. Close enough.
And then she stepped back.
Not away — she kept his hand. But back. One step.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I can't.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Daisy —

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I can't hold this and August at the same time, Kit. If I kiss you right now and then we leave for different states I don't — I don't know what I do with that. I don't know how to carry that.
He looked at her. Her hand was still in his.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Hey. Don't explain it.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: It's not — I want to. I need you to know I want to.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: So do I. Daisy, so do I.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: This isn't me saying no forever.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: That's not what I heard.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I just — I need a place for it. A real place. Not a maybe and then a goodbye.
He held her hand. The street was quiet. She was right. It still ached.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Okay.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Yeah?

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Yeah. We'll figure out the place.
She squeezed his hand once. He squeezed back. They started walking again — her walk, the end of the block — and they held hands the rest of the way home without making a thing of it.
At her porch she looked at him.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Goodnight, Kit.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Goodnight.
She went inside. He walked home alone in the quiet.
